target.title: fix(codex): deliver Telegram verbose tool progress
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram direct chats with /verbose enabled could show no Codex tool-call progress bubbles at all in message-tool-only/source-suppressed turns.
- Why it matters: users expect /verbose on to show the agent work between prompt and final answer, including the actual tool calls used to produce the answer.
- What changed: deliver verbose tool summaries for direct message-tool-only turns, emit transcript-backed dynamic tool summaries, and filter follow-up noise discovered while fixing this, including generic Working statuses and message/log-activity progress.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no changes to Telegram message chunking/splitting, model behavior, or channel delivery for room/channel message-tool-only turns.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #82917
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: /verbose on in a Telegram direct chat should show Codex tool-call progress without generic Working status messages, and should still deliver the final answer.
- Real environment tested: Hetzner VPS running OpenClaw gateway from this PR branch, Telegram direct chat with @SuperDuperChimpBot, Codex provider openai-codex/gpt-5.5.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: restarted the gateway against the built PR branch, then sent in Telegram: `PR branch verbose test: research with exa how to grow a pear tree`.
- Evidence after fix: Telegram screenshot from 2026-05-17 shows two `mcporter call 'exa.web_search_exa(...)'` tool-call bubbles, then a normal final answer. No generic `Working:` bubbles appear.
- Observed result after fix: verbose tool-call bubbles appeared in Telegram, generic Working noise was absent, and the final pear-tree answer arrived.
- What was not tested: non-Telegram channels in a live external environment; channel/group message-tool-only behavior was covered by unit tests.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): prior Telegram testing showed either no verbose tool calls on official beta, or noisy `Working: bash...` messages before this patch.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Codex could generate verbose tool summaries, but direct message-tool-only/source-suppressed dispatch paths returned before delivering those summaries to Telegram. Separately, transcript-recorded dynamic tools did not emit concise verbose summaries, while message/log-activity tools could produce noisy progress.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no regression test covered direct Telegram-style message-tool-only source suppression while /verbose was enabled.
- Contributing context (if known): #82917 restores snapshot-only Codex native tool progress generation; this PR complements it by ensuring the direct Telegram delivery path emits the verbose summaries.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`, `extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts`, `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: direct message-tool-only turns with /verbose enabled deliver tool summaries, suppress generic Working statuses, preserve room/channel suppression, and filter message/log-activity progress noise.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it directly covers the dispatch source-suppression branch and Codex projector transcript paths without requiring live Telegram.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none before this PR.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Telegram direct chats with /verbose enabled now show concise tool-call summaries in message-tool-only/source-suppressed turns. Generic `Working:` status messages are suppressed.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Telegram direct /verbose on -> sourceReplyDeliveryMode=message_tool_only -> tool summary generated -> suppressed before Telegram

After:
Telegram direct /verbose on -> sourceReplyDeliveryMode=message_tool_only -> direct verbose exception -> Telegram tool bubble -> final answer
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (No)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (No)
- New/changed network calls? (No)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (No)
- Data access scope changed? (No)
- If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux on Hetzner VPS
- Runtime/container: Node 24.14.1, OpenClaw gateway built from this branch
- Model/provider: openai-codex/gpt-5.5
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram direct chat
- Relevant config (redacted): /verbose enabled; source replies delivered through message-tool-only behavior

### Steps

1. Build this branch with `pnpm build`.
2. Run gateway from `dist/entry.js gateway --port 18789`.
3. In Telegram direct chat, ensure /verbose is enabled.
4. Send: `PR branch verbose test: research with exa how to grow a pear tree`.

### Expected

- Telegram shows tool-call progress bubbles.
- Telegram does not show generic `Working:` bubbles.
- Telegram receives the final answer.

### Actual

- Tool-call progress bubbles appeared for Exa searches.
- No generic `Working:` bubbles appeared.
- Final answer arrived.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Local validation run:

- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` - 3 files, 326 tests passed.
- `pnpm test:extension codex` - 54 files, 857 tests passed.
- `pnpm build` - passed.
- Targeted oxlint on touched core/extension files - passed.
- `codex review --base origin/main` - no actionable findings.

Notes:

- `pnpm format:check` currently reports unrelated formatting issues on current main outside this PR's touched files; the six touched files were formatted directly.
- `pnpm check:changed` passed guardrails and core/extension typechecks, but `lint core` was SIGKILLed by local resource pressure while running `tsgolint`.
- `pnpm test:contracts:channels` currently fails on unrelated `qa-channel` plugin metadata on current main.

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: live Telegram direct chat with /verbose enabled shows Exa tool-call bubbles, no generic Working messages, final answer delivered.
- Edge cases checked: unit tests cover room/channel message-tool-only suppression and message/log-activity noise suppression.
- What you did **not** verify: live Slack/Discord/Matrix behavior.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (Yes)
- Config/env changes? (No)
- Migration needed? (No)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: direct Telegram verbose turns could surface too much low-value progress.
  - Mitigation: generic Working messages and message/log-activity tools are filtered; room/channel message-tool-only suppression remains covered by tests.

target.title: fix(codex): surface declined native tool replies
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #83093 by preserving declined Codex native tool failures at the attempt boundary so aborted empty turns still produce a visible channel reply.

- Records non-success Codex native commandExecution / fileChange results as lastToolError.
- Marks mutating native tool failures so the existing payload warning policy surfaces them even with verbose output off.
- Adds regressions for declined native command projection and aborted empty payload emission.

## Validation

- CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/payloads.test.ts
- ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/payloads.test.ts && git diff --check
- pnpm check:changed

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: A Codex app-server native commandExecution item with status declined could terminate an external-channel turn with no assistant text and no lastToolError, so the payload builder emitted no visible reply.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu 24.04, with two fresh git worktrees: current upstream/main at 44c3d8ea2efd01d04b7ef861b54a5a46a05f4dfa and this PR head at 2b15eb6588464298b7441d5d0a26a89270b68ace.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran pr-83108/real-behavior-proof-runner.mts in both fresh worktrees. The runner imports and executes the production CodexAppServerEventProjector and production buildEmbeddedRunPayloads modules, feeds item/started, item/completed(status=declined), and turn/completed(status=interrupted) for a Codex-native bash commandExecution, then records the external-channel ReplyPayload objects the payload builder returns.
- Evidence after fix: ![Real before/after proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83108-fresh/pr-83108/codex-native-decline-reply-real-before-after.png) Raw artifacts: [summary](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83108-fresh/pr-83108/summary.txt), [before main JSON](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83108-fresh/pr-83108/before-main-real-app-server.json), [after PR JSON](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83108-fresh/pr-83108/after-pr-83108-real-app-server.json), [proof runner](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83108-fresh/pr-83108/real-behavior-proof-runner.mts)
- Observed result after fix: Before, current main projects the declined native command as an aborted attempt with lastToolError=null and buildEmbeddedRunPayloads returns zero external-channel payloads. After this PR, the projector records lastToolError={toolName:bash, error:codex native tool blocked, mutatingAction:true}, and buildEmbeddedRunPayloads returns one error ReplyPayload: "⚠️ 🛠️ run tests (in /workspace/openclaw) failed".
- What was not tested: A hosted Telegram/Slack transport was not used; the proof stops at the ReplyPayload handoff consumed by external channel transports.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The Codex app-server projector mapped declined native tools to blocked diagnostics, but did not carry the non-success native tool into EmbeddedRunAttemptResult.lastToolError.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no regression for an aborted empty Codex turn with a declined native command producing a user-visible payload.
- Contributing context: The payload layer already knows how to surface mutating tool failures, but it never received the native command failure summary.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this: Seam / integration test.
- Target test or file: extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts and src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/payloads.test.ts.
- Scenario the test should lock in: declined Codex native commandExecution produces lastToolError, and an aborted empty attempt with that error emits a visible Bash error payload.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It covers the projector-to-payload seam without requiring a live external channel or real native tool decline.

target.title: Telegram: wake on reaction events
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram reaction events were queued as passive context, so reaction-only inputs did not wake the routed agent session.
- Why it matters: operators can react to a bot message and expect OpenClaw to process that signal without sending a follow-up text message.
- What changed: accepted Telegram reaction events now include stable `reaction_key` values for standard and custom emoji and request an immediate heartbeat wake after the system event is successfully enqueued.
- Scope boundary: no new per-emoji semantics/config API is added; emoji meanings can be supplied through agent/user preference text instead of hardcoded channel logic.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #47677
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: accepted Telegram reaction-only events now wake the routed session after enqueue, and standard/custom emoji are exposed as stable reaction keys in the system event text/context key.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, branch rewritten onto `openclaw/main` commit `45a434fb23`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`
  - `pnpm tsgo:extensions && pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check CHANGELOG.md docs/channels/telegram.md extensions/telegram/src/bot-deps.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test-harness.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: focused Telegram Vitest passed `2 files, 138 tests`; extension source/test typechecks passed; format check passed.
- Observed result after fix: standard emoji reactions enqueue `reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>`, custom emoji reactions enqueue `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>`, and successfully enqueued reaction events call `requestHeartbeat({ source: "notifications-event", intent: "immediate", reason: "telegram-reaction", sessionKey })`.
- What was not tested: I did not send a live Telegram reaction through a production bot; maintainers/operators may still want redacted live Bot API proof before merge.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): previous PR body and issue #47677 described reaction events being queued but not processed until a later message.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Telegram `message_reaction` handling enqueued system events and then returned without requesting a heartbeat wake.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing tests asserted enqueue behavior but did not assert wake behavior for reaction-only inputs.
- Contributing context (if known): Telegram custom emoji reactions were not represented in the event text because only standard emoji reactions were collected.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: accepted standard/custom emoji reactions enqueue stable reaction-key events and wake only when enqueue succeeds.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the Telegram handler already has a focused harness for reaction authorization/routing/enqueue behavior.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing reaction enqueue tests covered notification routing but not wake behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Telegram reaction notifications that pass existing access controls now wake the routed session. System event text includes `reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>` or `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` so preference/prompt text can assign meaning to specific reactions without adding a channel config schema.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Telegram reaction] -> [system event queued] -> [processed only after later wake]

After:
[Telegram reaction] -> [system event queued] -> [heartbeat wake] -> [agent sees reaction event]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram handler unit harness
- Relevant config (redacted): `channels.telegram.reactionNotifications: "all"`, `dmPolicy: "open"`, `allowFrom: ["*"]`

### Steps

1. Run the focused Telegram reaction and dispatch tests.
2. Run extension source/test typechecks.
3. Run format check for touched files plus the Telegram deps type surface.

### Expected

- Reaction event tests pass, including heartbeat wake assertions.
- Typecheck and format checks pass.

### Actual

- Passed.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: standard emoji enqueue + wake, custom emoji stable key enqueue + wake, no wake when enqueue deduplicates, existing reaction authorization/routing cases.
- Edge cases checked: reaction removals ignored, `own`/`all`/`off` modes, bot-user reactions ignored, forum/general topic routing remains unchanged.
- What you did **not** verify: live Telegram Bot API delivery.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: reaction notifications can now start an agent turn where they previously waited for another wake.
  - Mitigation: behavior is still gated by existing `reactionNotifications` and Telegram sender/access controls, and duplicate queue entries do not wake.

commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Discord reaction notifications to include stable reaction keys, wake the routed session after a successful system-event enqueue, adds focused monitor tests, and updates Discord docs plus changelog.

target.title: Discord: wake on reaction events
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Discord reaction notifications were enqueued as passive system events, so reaction-only inputs could wait for a later wake before the agent processed them.
- Why it matters: Discord is a low-noise operator surface; a reaction should be enough to get the routed session's attention when reactions are already enabled and authorized.
- What changed: accepted Discord reaction events now include stable `reaction_key` values for standard/custom emoji and request an immediate heartbeat wake after the system event is successfully enqueued.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no per-emoji semantics/config API, no hardcoded emoji-to-action map, and no new Discord permissions. Emoji meanings can live in preferences/prompt text.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83164
- Related #47687
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: accepted Discord reaction-only events now wake the routed session after enqueue, and standard/custom emoji are exposed as stable reaction keys in the system event text/context key.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, branch based on `openclaw/main` commit `45a434fb23`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check CHANGELOG.md docs/channels/discord.md extensions/discord/src/monitor/listeners.reactions.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts`
  - `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
  - `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: focused Discord monitor Vitest passed `1 file, 46 tests`; extension typecheck passed; format check passed.
- Observed result after fix: standard emoji reactions enqueue `reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>`, custom emoji reactions enqueue `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>`, and successfully enqueued reaction events call `requestHeartbeat({ source: "notifications-event", intent: "immediate", reason: "discord-reaction", sessionKey })`.
- What was not tested: I did not send a live Discord reaction through a production bot; maintainers/operators may want redacted live gateway proof before merge.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): source inspection showed Discord reaction listeners enqueued system events without a heartbeat wake.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Discord reaction listener code enqueued system events and returned without requesting a heartbeat wake.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing tests asserted reaction enqueue/routing behavior but did not assert wake behavior for reaction-only inputs.
- Contributing context (if known): reaction event text included a formatted emoji label but not a stable key that preference text can target consistently.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: accepted standard/custom emoji reaction notifications enqueue stable reaction-key events and wake only when enqueue succeeds.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the Discord monitor harness already covers reaction notification auth, routing, and enqueue behavior.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing reaction tests covered notification enqueue/routing but not heartbeat wake.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Discord reaction notifications that pass existing access controls now wake the routed session. System event text includes `reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>` or `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` so preferences/prompt text can assign meaning to specific reactions without channel-specific semantics config.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Discord reaction] -> [system event queued] -> [processed only after later wake]

After:
[Discord reaction] -> [system event queued] -> [heartbeat wake] -> [agent sees reaction event]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord monitor unit harness
- Relevant config (redacted): default DM reaction test config and guild `reactionNotifications` cases

### Steps

1. Run the focused Discord monitor tests.
2. Run extension typecheck.
3. Run format check for touched files.

### Expected

- Reaction event tests pass, including heartbeat wake assertions.
- Typecheck and format checks pass.

### Actual

- Passed.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: standard emoji enqueue + wake, custom emoji stable key enqueue + wake, no wake when enqueue deduplicates, existing reaction authorization/routing cases.
- Edge cases checked: blocked DM/guild/group reactions remain blocked, `off`/`own`/`all`/`allowlist` modes still gate behavior, custom emoji context keys are stable.
- What you did **not** verify: live Discord Gateway delivery.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: reaction notifications can now start an agent turn where they previously waited for another wake.
  - Mitigation: behavior is still gated by existing `reactionNotifications` and Discord sender/channel/guild access controls, and duplicate queue entries do not wake.

target.title: Forward cron toolsAllow to CLI runs
target.body:
## Summary
- Forward isolated cron payload `toolsAllow` into the CLI runner path.
- Keep CLI/native-tool backends fail-closed when a cron job requests a runtime allowlist.
- Add a regression test covering a cron agent turn with `toolsAllow: ["web_fetch", "message"]`.

## Root cause
The embedded cron runner already received `agentPayload.toolsAllow`, but the CLI runner branch dropped it while still passing the normal skill snapshot and prompt context. CLI backends already reject runtime allowlists when they cannot enforce them, so the missing handoff let restricted cron jobs continue through a native-tool harness without the intended policy check.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Isolated cron jobs that specify `toolsAllow` must pass that runtime allowlist into the CLI runner path instead of letting a native CLI harness run without the intended restriction.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw branch `codex/cron-cli-toolsallow-fail-closed` on macOS, launched from temp HOME `/tmp/openclaw-pr82416-proof` with gateway port `19876` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr82416-proof/.openclaw/openclaw.json`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Started `pnpm openclaw gateway --dev --port 19876 --auth none --allow-unconfigured --compact`; added a real cron job with `pnpm openclaw cron add --name cli-toolsallow-proof --every 1h --session isolated --message "fetch weather" --model claude-cli/opus --tools web_fetch,message --json`; manually ran it with `pnpm openclaw cron run beba7234-2487-4378-8744-a0fd3e018454 --expect-final --timeout 30000`; queried the result with `pnpm openclaw cron runs --id beba7234-2487-4378-8744-a0fd3e018454 --limit 5 --expect-final --timeout 30000`.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from the real local run:

```text
OpenClaw 2026.5.16 (a4f044d)
gateway listening on ws://127.0.0.1:19876
cron job payload: model=claude-cli/opus, toolsAllow=["web_fetch", "message"]

openclaw cron runs --id beba7234-2487-4378-8744-a0fd3e018454 --limit 5 --expect-final --timeout 30000
status: error
error: Error: CLI backend claude-cli cannot enforce runtime toolsAllow; use an embedded runtime for restricted tool policy
diagnostics.summary: CLI backend claude-cli cannot enforce runtime toolsAllow; use an embedded runtime for restricted tool policy
runId: manual:beba7234-2487-4378-8744-a0fd3e018454:1778902107983:1
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The runtime allowlist reaches the CLI path and the CLI backend fails closed before a native CLI harness can run with unrestricted tools.
- **What was not tested**: Nothing else.

## Validation
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/cron/isolated-agent/run.skill-filter.test.ts --maxWorkers=1`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.skill-filter.test.ts`

target.title: fix(lmstudio): resolve env-template API keys
target.body:
## Summary

- Resolve LM Studio configured `apiKey` strings through the standard SecretInput path before falling back to known env-marker handling.
- Fix arbitrary env-template keys like `${LMSTUDIO_API_KEY}` being treated as literal API keys.
- Preserve higher-precedence auth when an optional configured `apiKey` env template is unset, including `headers.Authorization` and resolved discovery/profile auth.
- Add LM Studio runtime and discovery regressions for custom env-template API keys, unresolved-template errors, header-auth precedence, and discovery-key precedence.

Refs #80495. This addresses the auth-resolution portion only; native `/api/v1/chat` inference fallback remains separate provider/API scope.

## Reported Repro

- #80495 reports an LM Studio config using an env-backed API key where OpenClaw sent the env var marker/name as the bearer token instead of the resolved value.
- Source inspection matched that report: arbitrary `${ENV}` apiKey templates did not go through the generic SecretInput resolution path unless the env var name was a registered provider marker.

## After-Fix Proof

- `pnpm test extensions/lmstudio/src/runtime.test.ts extensions/lmstudio/src/setup.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` passed: 2 files, 53 tests.
- The focused regressions prove `${LMSTUDIO_API_KEY}` resolves to the configured env value before auth header construction.
- The regressions prove unresolved `${LMSTUDIO_API_KEY}` still reports a path-specific error when no other auth satisfies the provider.
- The regressions prove `headers.Authorization` continues to satisfy auth when `${LMSTUDIO_API_KEY}` is unset, covering both runtime auth and discovery/setup behavior.
- The regressions prove a resolved `discoveryApiKey` continues to take precedence over a stale unset configured `apiKey` template during provider discovery.

No live LM Studio server verification was run for this branch.

## Verification

- `pnpm test extensions/lmstudio/src/runtime.test.ts extensions/lmstudio/src/setup.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:extensions`
- `pnpm format:check -- extensions/lmstudio/src/setup.ts extensions/lmstudio/src/setup.test.ts extensions/lmstudio/src/runtime.ts extensions/lmstudio/src/runtime.test.ts CHANGELOG.md`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/lmstudio/src/setup.ts extensions/lmstudio/src/setup.test.ts extensions/lmstudio/src/runtime.ts extensions/lmstudio/src/runtime.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

target.title: fix(memory): skip placeholder short-term promotions
target.body:
Summary
- Add a shared short-term promotion guard for markdown placeholder/skeleton snippets.
- Apply it when loading the recall store, recording recall/grounded candidates, ranking, relocating, applying, and rehydrating promotion candidates.
- Add regression coverage for marker-only snippets, heading-plus-empty-bullet skeletons, normalized marker-plus-heading placeholders, stale stored placeholders, and direct apply rejection while preserving ordinary short bullet content.

Fixes #80582.

Verification
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts -- --runInBand
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts
- git diff --check

Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Short-term promotion could treat markdown placeholders like `-`, heading-plus-empty-bullet skeletons, and previously persisted normalized skeleton snippets like `- ## Entscheidungen` as durable memory candidates, allowing empty daily-note scaffolding to rank and eventually append to `MEMORY.md`.

Real environment tested: Local filesystem-backed memory-core promotion fixtures on macOS, using the repo Vitest wrapper. The tests create real temp workspace files under `memory/`, write the short-term recall store, call the production record/rank/apply functions, and inspect the resulting `MEMORY.md` behavior.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts -- --runInBand`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts`; `git diff --check`.

Evidence after fix: The focused regression tests pass: a grounded candidate whose snippet is `-` records no promotable candidate, an already-stored normalized placeholder entry whose snippet is `- ## Entscheidungen` does not rank, and a direct apply attempt with that snippet does not create `MEMORY.md`. The full memory-core dreaming/promotion proof passed with 2 test files and 98 tests.

Observed result after fix: Placeholder snippets are rejected at record, store-load, rank, relocation, apply, and rehydration boundaries. A real bullet containing durable text, `- Keep gateway restarts supervised.`, remains promotable.

What was not tested: I did not run a full live dreaming cron cycle against a private real workspace; the user-reported failure shape is covered with filesystem-backed promotion fixtures rather than private memory contents.

target.title: fix(discord): return subagent thread delivery origin
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Discord delegated sessions can bind a thread successfully but still post the initial child reply into the parent channel.
- Why it matters: thread-bound delegation looks broken even when the binding exists, weakening isolation for delegated work.
- What changed: the Discord `subagent_spawning` hook now returns a routable Discord `deliveryOrigin` after `autoBindSpawnedDiscordSubagent` succeeds, and the hook test asserts that contract.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no generic spawn-path refactor, no session-store schema change, and no non-Discord behavior change.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83170
- Related #42986
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord thread-bound `sessions_spawn` accepted a child session and created the binding, but the initial child reply still landed in the parent channel.
- Real environment tested: local managed OpenClaw runtime on macOS with Discord thread bindings enabled, plus this local source checkout.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/discord/src/subagent-hooks.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): targeted hook regression test passes in this source checkout.
- Observed result after fix: the Discord hook returns a routable `deliveryOrigin` for the bound thread in the covered test path.
- What was not tested: full live end-to-end child delivery from this exact source checkout after the patch.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): live managed-runtime repro before the patch showed `sessions_spawn(thread=true, mode=session)` accepted while the child still resolved to the parent `#general` channel.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `extensions/discord/src/subagent-hooks.ts` created the thread binding but returned only `threadBindingReady: true`. The generic spawn path only switches the initial child run onto the thread when `subagent_spawning` also returns a routable `deliveryOrigin`.
- Missing detection / guardrail: the Discord hook test asserted binding readiness but did not assert the returned delivery target.
- Contributing context (if known): the completion-delivery hook already knew how to derive a bound Discord thread target, but the initial child run path depended on the spawning hook.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/discord/src/subagent-hooks.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: when Discord `subagent_spawning` successfully binds a thread, it must return `deliveryOrigin` pointing at the bound Discord thread.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the failure is in the Discord hook contract, not in generic spawn orchestration.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): the existing bind-on-spawn test in the same file, now extended.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Discord thread-bound delegated child sessions can route their initial reply into the bound thread instead of falling back to the parent channel.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Discord sessions_spawn(thread=true) -> thread binding created -> initial child reply falls back to parent channel

After:
Discord sessions_spawn(thread=true) -> thread binding created -> deliveryOrigin returned -> initial child reply routes to bound thread
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local source checkout plus local managed OpenClaw runtime repro
- Model/provider: `openai/gpt-5.4` in the observed repro
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord
- Relevant config (redacted): Discord thread bindings enabled; `spawnSubagentSessions` enabled

### Steps

1. Enable Discord thread bindings for delegated spawns.
2. Call `sessions_spawn` with `thread=true` and `mode=session` from a Discord channel.
3. Observe where the initial child reply lands.

### Expected

- Initial child reply lands in the bound Discord thread.

### Actual

- Before fix, the initial child reply fell back to the parent channel even though binding succeeded.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: traced the live repro in the managed runtime; verified the hook-level regression test passes after the patch.
- Edge cases checked: scope stays Discord-only and does not alter the generic spawn path.
- What you did **not** verify: live end-to-end delivery from this exact source checkout after the patch.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

target.title: fix(memory): preserve qmd lexical search for hyphenated queries
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve the raw user query for QMD lexical (`lex`) searches so dashed identifiers, dates, and version strings can still match exactly.
- Normalize only QMD semantic sub-searches (`vec` / `hyde`) by replacing word-internal hyphens with spaces before sending the v2 `qmd.query` searches array.
- Add focused coverage for hybrid and vector-only QMD MCP payloads.

Fixes #81328.

## Tests

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts --reporter=verbose -t "hyphenated tokens|vector-only QMD"`
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts --reporter=verbose -t "falls back to builtin search when qmd fails with sqlite busy|keeps original qmd error when fallback manager initialization fails"`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check`

Full `qmd-manager.test.ts` run on Windows reached 104/105 passing; the remaining failure was the existing symlink-permission case (`EPERM` creating a symlink), unrelated to this change.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: `memory_search` should not lose QMD lexical recall just because a query contains hyphenated tokens such as `sqlite-vec`, `2026-05-04`, or `multi-agent`, while semantic QMD searches should avoid word-internal hyphens that QMD v2.0.1 treats as NOT-operator syntax.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu-24.04 source worktrees created from `upstream/main` and PR head `0f7460c4d99d028e07a629bfd178afc105a6c308`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: created clean detached worktrees for `upstream/main` and `fork/fix-qmd-hyphenated-memory-search`; in each worktree ran `node --import ./node_modules/tsx/dist/loader.mjs ./probe-qmd-hyphenated-search.mjs`. The probe imported the real `QmdMemoryManager` and called the real `buildV2Searches()` payload-construction seam for hybrid `query` and vector-only `vsearch` payloads.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

![PR #81423 QMD hyphenated search before/after proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-81423-fresh/pr-81423/pr-81423-qmd-hyphenated-search-before-after-proof.png)

```text
Fresh proof artifact: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-81423-fresh/pr-81423/pr-81423-qmd-hyphenated-search-before-after-proof.png
Proof summary: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-81423-fresh/pr-81423/summary.txt

BEFORE upstream/main:
status=0
lexPreserved=true
semanticNormalized=false
vectorOnlyNormalized=false
lexQuery=sqlite-vec backend health 2026-05-04 multi-agent
vecQuery=sqlite-vec backend health 2026-05-04 multi-agent
hydeQuery=sqlite-vec backend health 2026-05-04 multi-agent
vectorOnlyQuery=sqlite-vec backend health
ok=false

AFTER PR #81423 head:
status=0
lexPreserved=true
semanticNormalized=true
vectorOnlyNormalized=true
lexQuery=sqlite-vec backend health 2026-05-04 multi-agent
vecQuery=sqlite vec backend health 2026 05 04 multi agent
hydeQuery=sqlite vec backend health 2026 05 04 multi agent
vectorOnlyQuery=sqlite vec backend health
ok=true
```

- Observed result after fix: `upstream/main` preserves the lexical query but sends hyphenated text to semantic `vec`, `hyde`, and vector-only payloads. PR head preserves the raw lexical branch while normalizing only semantic payloads, so exact lexical recall remains available and QMD semantic validation no longer sees word-internal hyphens.
- What was not tested: no live QMD 2.0.1 daemon or full `memory_search` command against an installed QMD index was run in this environment. The proof verifies the runtime payload-construction seam that feeds QMD MCP calls.

target.title: Forward cron toolsAllow to CLI runs
target.body:
## Summary
- Forward isolated cron payload `toolsAllow` into the CLI runner path.
- Keep CLI/native-tool backends fail-closed when a cron job requests a runtime allowlist.
- Add a regression test covering a cron agent turn with `toolsAllow: ["web_fetch", "message"]`.

## Root cause
The embedded cron runner already received `agentPayload.toolsAllow`, but the CLI runner branch dropped it while still passing the normal skill snapshot and prompt context. CLI backends already reject runtime allowlists when they cannot enforce them, so the missing handoff let restricted cron jobs continue through a native-tool harness without the intended policy check.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Isolated cron jobs that specify `toolsAllow` must pass that runtime allowlist into the CLI runner path instead of letting a native CLI harness run without the intended restriction.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw branch `codex/cron-cli-toolsallow-fail-closed` on macOS, launched from temp HOME `/tmp/openclaw-pr82416-proof` with gateway port `19876` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr82416-proof/.openclaw/openclaw.json`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Started `pnpm openclaw gateway --dev --port 19876 --auth none --allow-unconfigured --compact`; added a real cron job with `pnpm openclaw cron add --name cli-toolsallow-proof --every 1h --session isolated --message "fetch weather" --model claude-cli/opus --tools web_fetch,message --json`; manually ran it with `pnpm openclaw cron run beba7234-2487-4378-8744-a0fd3e018454 --expect-final --timeout 30000`; queried the result with `pnpm openclaw cron runs --id beba7234-2487-4378-8744-a0fd3e018454 --limit 5 --expect-final --timeout 30000`.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from the real local run:

```text
OpenClaw 2026.5.16 (a4f044d)
gateway listening on ws://127.0.0.1:19876
cron job payload: model=claude-cli/opus, toolsAllow=["web_fetch", "message"]

openclaw cron runs --id beba7234-2487-4378-8744-a0fd3e018454 --limit 5 --expect-final --timeout 30000
status: error
error: Error: CLI backend claude-cli cannot enforce runtime toolsAllow; use an embedded runtime for restricted tool policy
diagnostics.summary: CLI backend claude-cli cannot enforce runtime toolsAllow; use an embedded runtime for restricted tool policy
runId: manual:beba7234-2487-4378-8744-a0fd3e018454:1778902107983:1
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The runtime allowlist reaches the CLI path and the CLI backend fails closed before a native CLI harness can run with unrestricted tools.
- **What was not tested**: Nothing else.

## Validation
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/cron/isolated-agent/run.skill-filter.test.ts --maxWorkers=1`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.skill-filter.test.ts`

target.title: Memory/QMD: isolate mcporter sidecars per agent
target.body:
## Summary

- Isolate QMD mcporter daemon/config state per agent by writing an agent-scoped mcporter config and passing it to mcporter calls.
- Preserve the configured mcporter server definition instead of replacing custom/remote server config with a hard-coded local `qmd mcp` entry.
- Keep QMD model/env settings flowing only through the generated stdio server entry, while stripping QMD-scoped XDG env from mcporter's own process environment.
- Tolerate mcporter daemon log lines before JSON responses so warm daemon calls do not break memory search parsing.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
- `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** QMD memory search through mcporter must keep each agent on its own mcporter config/daemon state while preserving the configured `qmd` mcporter server and avoiding QMD-scoped XDG env leakage into mcporter's own process env.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux with Node `v24.15.0`, `mcporter 0.10.2`, `qmd 2.1.0`, configured agents `hex` and `trump`, and the configured mcporter `qmd` stdio server. Home paths are redacted as `~`; private snippets are omitted.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```text
node --version
mcporter --version
qmd --version
mcporter config get qmd --json
pnpm openclaw memory status --deep --agent hex --json
pnpm openclaw memory status --deep --agent trump --json
pnpm --silent openclaw memory search --agent hex --json --max-results 1 "OpenClaw"
pnpm --silent openclaw memory search --agent trump --json --max-results 1 "OpenClaw"
node -e '<read generated ~/.openclaw/agents/{hex,trump}/qmd/mcporter/mcporter.json and print redacted command/env summary>'
```

**Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output, redacted:

```text
node --version -> v24.15.0
mcporter --version -> 0.10.2
qmd --version -> qmd 2.1.0

mcporter config get qmd --json
{
  "name": "qmd",
  "source": { "kind": "local", "path": "~/.mcporter/mcporter.json" },
  "transport": "stdio",
  "command": "~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin/qmd",
  "args": ["mcp"]
}

pnpm openclaw memory status --deep --agent hex --json
  agentId=hex backend=qmd files=1310 chunks=1310 dirty=false vector.available=true vector.semanticAvailable=true embeddingProbe.ok=true collections=4

pnpm openclaw memory status --deep --agent trump --json
  agentId=trump backend=qmd files=204 chunks=204 dirty=false vector.available=true vector.semanticAvailable=true embeddingProbe.ok=true collections=4

pnpm --silent openclaw memory search --agent hex --json --max-results 1 "OpenClaw"
  exited 0; results.length=1; first.source=memory; first.score=0.93

pnpm --silent openclaw memory search --agent trump --json --max-results 1 "OpenClaw"
  exited 0; results.length=1; first.source=sessions; first.score=0.93

Generated mcporter config summaries:
hex:   path=~/.openclaw/agents/hex/qmd/mcporter/mcporter.json   imports=[] command=~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin/qmd args=["mcp"] XDG_CONFIG_HOME=~/.openclaw/agents/hex/qmd/xdg-config QMD_CONFIG_DIR=~/.openclaw/agents/hex/qmd/xdg-config/qmd XDG_CACHE_HOME=~/.openclaw/agents/hex/qmd/xdg-cache
trump: path=~/.openclaw/agents/trump/qmd/mcporter/mcporter.json imports=[] command=~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin/qmd args=["mcp"] XDG_CONFIG_HOME=~/.openclaw/agents/trump/qmd/xdg-config QMD_CONFIG_DIR=~/.openclaw/agents/trump/qmd/xdg-config/qmd XDG_CACHE_HOME=~/.openclaw/agents/trump/qmd/xdg-cache
```

**Observed result after fix:** Both agents reported healthy QMD vector readiness (`embeddingProbe.ok=true`, `vector.available=true`, `vector.semanticAvailable=true`), both `memory search` commands returned one result successfully, and the generated mcporter configs were separate per agent while preserving the configured `qmd` stdio command.

**What was not tested:** I did not test a live remote HTTP mcporter server; that preservation path is covered by the focused unit test added in this PR.

target.title: fix(compaction): bound stale transcript usage
target.body:
Fixes #81178

## Summary
- bound stale raw transcript prompt-usage snapshots against the recent active replay estimate
- keep post-usage tail pressure additive for fresh usage records so interrupted tool output after the latest usage record remains conservative
- when a post-usage compaction marker proves the latest usage snapshot is stale, count only the post-marker tail bytes instead of stale pre-compaction tail bytes
- add a preflight compaction regression for a stale 240k-token usage record, a 450k-byte stale pre-compaction tool result, and a small post-compaction replay

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: stale pre-compaction transcript usage and stale pre-marker tail bytes should not force another preflight compaction when the active post-compaction replay is small and no explicit transcript-byte policy is exceeded.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source runtime from PR head `27b6d972dd` on macOS, using a real temporary JSONL session transcript and the actual `runPreflightCompactionIfNeeded` path. The compact dependency was instrumented only to count whether the runtime attempted compaction.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: from the PR checkout at `27b6d972dd`, ran a `node --import tsx` source-runtime proof that wrote a transcript with stale assistant usage `input=240000`, stale assistant usage `output=120000`, a 450033-byte pre-marker tool result, a compaction marker, and a small post-compaction replay; then it invoked `runPreflightCompactionIfNeeded` with a 100000-token context window.

Evidence after fix: copied terminal output from the direct source-runtime proof:

```json
{
  "sourceRuntime": "runPreflightCompactionIfNeeded",
  "prHead": "27b6d972dd",
  "staleTranscriptUsageInput": 240000,
  "staleTranscriptUsageOutput": 120000,
  "preMarkerToolTextBytes": 450033,
  "contextWindowTokens": 100000,
  "returnedOriginalSessionEntry": true,
  "compactCalls": 0,
  "avoidedPreflightCompaction": true,
  "sessionFile": "/var/folders/qg/ftvdj43s1b79kv3g92r9yzsm0000gn/T/openclaw-81916-proof-0FMFBF/stale-tail-after-compaction.jsonl"
}
```

Observed result after fix: the source-runtime preflight call returned the original session entry and made zero compaction calls, despite the stale usage record plus stale pre-marker tail being larger than the configured 100k context window. The code still preserves fresh latest-usage tail pressure through the focused regression suite.

What was not tested: I did not run a live Discord/Pi provider session because the failure is in the local preflight estimator and the source-runtime proof exercises that estimator path directly.

## Verification
- `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts -t "drops stale transcript usage and pre-compaction tail"`

target.title: fix(heartbeat): clear pendingFinalDelivery* on send success
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #83184.

The heartbeat send path in `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts:2011-2022` wrote `lastHeartbeatText` / `lastHeartbeatSentAt` after a successful `sendDurableMessageBatch` but never nulled the `pendingFinalDelivery*` recovery fields. A heartbeat-driven agent run that previously set `pendingFinalDelivery: true` (via `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts:2169`) therefore left the session permanently stuck: subsequent heartbeats short-circuited in get-reply's redelivery path, the agent was never re-invoked, and the symptom surfaced as silent loss of notifications.

This change extends the existing post-success `updateSessionStore` block to clear all seven `pendingFinalDelivery*` fields, mirroring the canonical `clearPendingFinalDeliveryAfterSuccess` helper at `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts:365-391` (already invoked from the user-message dispatch path at `:1671`).

Affected versions: verified on `2026.5.7` through `2026.5.16-beta.2`; fix applies against current `main` (`2026.5.17`).

## Verification

Commands run locally against this branch on Ubuntu 24.04, Node 22.22.2, pnpm 11.1.0:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/heartbeat-runner.clears-pending-final-delivery.test.ts` — new regression test, 1 passed (also confirmed RED before the fix landed in the same branch).
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs <all 16 src/infra/heartbeat-runner.*.test.ts non-e2e files>` — 16 files / 152 tests passed.
- `pnpm check:changed` — green: conflict markers, changelog attributions, wildcard re-export guards, duplicate coverage, dependency pin guard, package patch guard, typecheck core, typecheck core tests, lint core (0/0), media download helper guard, runtime sidecar loader guard, runtime import cycles, webhook body guard, pairing store guard, pairing account guard.
- `oxfmt --check` and core oxlint — both clean on the two touched files.

The new test seeds a session with `pendingFinalDelivery: true` + a heartbeat-ack `pendingFinalDeliveryText` (so the heartbeat-defer check at `heartbeat-runner.ts:~1328` does not short-circuit), manually patches in the remaining five `pendingFinalDelivery*` fields the seeder does not expose, runs `runHeartbeatOnce` with a substantive reply, and asserts all seven fields are `undefined` afterward. It also asserts `lastHeartbeatText` / `lastHeartbeatSentAt` are written, proving the success branch was actually taken.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: heartbeat send-success path leaves `pendingFinalDelivery*` set, causing the next heartbeat to skip the agent via the get-reply redelivery short-circuit.

Real environment tested: Docker-deployed gateway, versions `2026.5.7` through `2026.5.16-beta.2`. The deterministic recurrence of the bug was confirmed there by an out-of-band cron task that nulls the stuck fields whenever `pendingFinalDeliveryText` matches `lastHeartbeatText`; the cron fires after every active-hours heartbeat that produces substantive text.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: applied the diff in this PR to a fresh checkout of `main` (`2026.5.17`); ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/heartbeat-runner.clears-pending-final-delivery.test.ts` (new regression test), then the full non-e2e `heartbeat-runner.*` test set, then `pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix:
- New regression test (`src/infra/heartbeat-runner.clears-pending-final-delivery.test.ts`) goes RED on `main` (`expected true to be undefined` at the `pendingFinalDelivery` assertion) and GREEN with this patch.
- Sister tests in `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.*` (16 files / 152 tests) remain green.
- `pnpm check:changed` exits 0; `oxfmt --check` and oxlint are clean on the touched files.

Observed result after fix: `runHeartbeatOnce` now nulls all seven `pendingFinalDelivery*` fields in the same `updateSessionStore` write that records `lastHeartbeatText` / `lastHeartbeatSentAt`. The subsequent-heartbeat short-circuit cannot trigger from prior heartbeat-driven runs.

What was not tested: an end-to-end heartbeat against a real channel was not exercised in this PR (the regression test stubs `getReplyFromConfig` and the outbound send). Production confirmation will follow from the deployment side once a heartbeat cycle runs through the patched code path.

target.title: Render Slack progress drafts as plan cards
target.body:
## Summary
- Preserve Slack native plan/task-card rendering for progress mode instead of falling back to legacy section/fields blocks.
- Stream native Slack `plan_update` / `task_update` chunks for progress events, including single-tool and concurrent progress updates.
- Use the newest meaningful progress step as the native plan title when no explicit label is configured, while preserving explicit configured labels.
- Finalize in-flight native progress tasks so completed replies do not leave stale `Running` cards.

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/progress-blocks.test.ts extensions/slack/src/streaming.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.streaming.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --pretty false`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --pretty false`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Slack native progress/task-card rendering remains on the native plan path, source-run streams native Slack progress while work is executing, and finalization clears running task state.
Real environment tested: local source-run OpenClaw gateway from this PR branch, Slack workspace `4ok`, channel `C0ASNARACMT`, infra-engineer-originated message mentioning prod Jules; gateway config used `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` through the source-run Codex auth store.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: restarted the local source-run gateway from `/home/simon/Workspace/openclaw-slack-plan-progress-drafts`, verified `/healthz`, then posted an infra-engineer Slack message with marker `native-progress-complete-e2e-20260516-154928` asking Jules to run exactly `python3 -c "import time; [print('progress-step-%d' % i, flush=True) or time.sleep(3) for i in range(1,6)]; print('FINAL_MARKER native-progress-complete-e2e-20260516-154928')"`; then polled `conversations.replies`, source-run session/trajectory files, source-run gateway log, and `/healthz` after completion.
Evidence after fix: copied live output from the source-run Slack Web API/session-log proof:
```text
parent_ts=1778946569.986069
reply_ts=1778946586.604129
marker=FINAL_MARKER native-progress-complete-e2e-20260516-154928
slack_block_type=plan
task_card={"type":"task_card","task_id":"bash_1","title":"Bash — Bash","status":"complete"}
final_seen=true
plan_message_count=1
stale_running_tasks=[]
false_error_tasks=[]
healthz_before={"ok":true,"status":"live"}
healthz_after={"ok":true,"status":"live"}
```
Slack replies for parent ts `1778946569.986069` included one native Slack `plan` block in Jules' reply ts `1778946586.604129`; the plan contained the native task card above; the final reply text contained `FINAL_MARKER native-progress-complete-e2e-20260516-154928`.
Observed result after fix: source-run logs showed Slack inbound for the marker plus `slack-stream` start/appends/stop in channel `C0ASNARACMT` thread `1778946569.986069`; the source-run trajectory ended `status: success` with provider/model `codex/gpt-5.5` / `openai-codex-responses`; no duplicate native progress plan messages were observed, no stale `running`/`in_progress` task remained after completion, and gateway health was `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}` before and after the E2E.
What was not tested: no screenshot/video capture of the transient Slack UI; proof is Slack Web API block state plus source-run gateway logs, session trajectory, and health checks.

target.title: fix(compaction): bound stale transcript usage
target.body:
Fixes #81178

## Summary
- bound stale raw transcript prompt-usage snapshots against the recent active replay estimate
- keep post-usage tail pressure additive for fresh usage records so interrupted tool output after the latest usage record remains conservative
- when a post-usage compaction marker proves the latest usage snapshot is stale, count only the post-marker tail bytes instead of stale pre-compaction tail bytes
- add a preflight compaction regression for a stale 240k-token usage record, a 450k-byte stale pre-compaction tool result, and a small post-compaction replay

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: stale pre-compaction transcript usage and stale pre-marker tail bytes should not force another preflight compaction when the active post-compaction replay is small and no explicit transcript-byte policy is exceeded.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source runtime from PR head `27b6d972dd` on macOS, using a real temporary JSONL session transcript and the actual `runPreflightCompactionIfNeeded` path. The compact dependency was instrumented only to count whether the runtime attempted compaction.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: from the PR checkout at `27b6d972dd`, ran a `node --import tsx` source-runtime proof that wrote a transcript with stale assistant usage `input=240000`, stale assistant usage `output=120000`, a 450033-byte pre-marker tool result, a compaction marker, and a small post-compaction replay; then it invoked `runPreflightCompactionIfNeeded` with a 100000-token context window.

Evidence after fix: copied terminal output from the direct source-runtime proof:

```json
{
  "sourceRuntime": "runPreflightCompactionIfNeeded",
  "prHead": "27b6d972dd",
  "staleTranscriptUsageInput": 240000,
  "staleTranscriptUsageOutput": 120000,
  "preMarkerToolTextBytes": 450033,
  "contextWindowTokens": 100000,
  "returnedOriginalSessionEntry": true,
  "compactCalls": 0,
  "avoidedPreflightCompaction": true,
  "sessionFile": "/var/folders/qg/ftvdj43s1b79kv3g92r9yzsm0000gn/T/openclaw-81916-proof-0FMFBF/stale-tail-after-compaction.jsonl"
}
```

Observed result after fix: the source-runtime preflight call returned the original session entry and made zero compaction calls, despite the stale usage record plus stale pre-marker tail being larger than the configured 100k context window. The code still preserves fresh latest-usage tail pressure through the focused regression suite.

What was not tested: I did not run a live Discord/Pi provider session because the failure is in the local preflight estimator and the source-runtime proof exercises that estimator path directly.

## Verification
- `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts -t "drops stale transcript usage and pre-compaction tail"`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 pnpm check:changed`

target.title: fix(gateway): support pinned daemon runtime paths
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: managed gateway/node services could start with Homebrew or PATH-selected runtimes instead of the operator-selected mise/Bun executable.
- Why it matters: launchd/systemd/schtasks do not load interactive shell startup files, so service restarts can drift away from the runtime that works in the user's shell.
- What changed: added `--runtime-path <path>` for gateway and node installs, validates absolute Node/Bun executables, persists `OPENCLAW_DAEMON_RUNTIME_PATH`, and preserves it through forced reinstall, start repair, update, and doctor repair flows.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no default runtime change, no shell profile mutation, no bundled runtime, and no changelog entry from this contributor PR.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: gateway/node service install plans can pin the exact runtime executable, including mise Node, and preserve that pin across repair/reinstall paths.
Real environment tested: local macOS source checkout on branch `fix/gateway-runtime-path-install`; post-rebase proof against `origin/main`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/program-args.test.ts src/commands/daemon-install-plan.shared.test.ts src/commands/daemon-install-helpers.test.ts src/commands/node-daemon-install-helpers.test.ts src/daemon/service-env.test.ts src/daemon/service-audit.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/register-service-commands.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/install.test.ts src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts`; `pnpm check:changed`; `pnpm format:docs:check`; `git diff --check`; `codex review --uncommitted`.
Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed 10 files / 230 tests; `pnpm check:changed` completed with typecheck, lint, import-cycle, runtime sidecar, webhook, and pairing guards green; docs formatting reported `Docs formatting clean (628 files)`; `git diff --check` passed; final Codex review had no remaining accepted/actionable findings.
Observed result after fix: runtime path validation, persistence, explicit runtime override, wrapper-managed Bun detection, service env pass-through, service audit suppression for explicit pins, and doctor repair preservation are covered by tests.
What was not tested: no live launchd/systemd/schtasks service was installed from this branch during PR prep.
Before evidence (optional but encouraged): original local failure showed launchd using `/opt/homebrew/bin/node` while the desired runtime was mise Node, causing the gateway to fail before listening.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: managed service launchers selected runtime from service manager environment/PATH or preferred system Node, while the user's working runtime lived behind an interactive version manager.
- Missing detection / guardrail: service install metadata had wrapper preservation but no generic persisted runtime executable contract.
- Contributing context (if known): `--runtime` selected only the runtime family; it could not express the exact executable path, and doctor repair could replace version-manager paths with system Node.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/daemon/program-args.test.ts`, `src/commands/daemon-install-helpers.test.ts`, `src/commands/node-daemon-install-helpers.test.ts`, `src/cli/daemon-cli/install.test.ts`, `src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts`, `src/daemon/service-audit.test.ts`, `src/daemon/service-env.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: absolute runtime path validation, Node version checks, Bun support, env persistence, forced reinstall preservation, explicit `--runtime` override of stale pins, wrapper-managed runtime inference, and doctor repair preservation.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these flows build the service plans and audit/repair decisions without mutating the host supervisor.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none for a generic persisted runtime executable path.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users can now run:

```bash
openclaw gateway install --runtime node --runtime-path "$(mise which node)" --force
openclaw node install --runtime node --runtime-path "$(mise which node)" --force
```

`--runtime-path` must be an absolute executable path matching the selected runtime. Node paths are checked for OpenClaw's minimum supported Node version.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
openclaw gateway install --runtime node -> service manager PATH/runtime -> possible drift

After:
openclaw gateway install --runtime node --runtime-path <node> -> persisted service command/env -> reinstall/doctor preserve selected runtime
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: the new CLI option controls the service executable path. It is mitigated by requiring an absolute executable file, matching the selected runtime family, and checking Node version before writing the service plan.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS local source checkout
- Runtime/container: Node via repo wrappers; service behavior verified through install plan/audit/repair tests
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): gateway/node managed service install paths
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Install/reinstall gateway with `--runtime node --runtime-path "$(mise which node)" --force`.
2. Restart or repair the managed service.
3. Inspect generated service command/env and doctor behavior.

### Expected

- Service command uses the pinned runtime executable.
- Service env persists `OPENCLAW_DAEMON_RUNTIME_PATH`.
- Forced reinstall, start repair, and doctor repair preserve the pin unless an explicit `--runtime` override is requested.

### Actual

- Verified by focused tests and changed gate in this PR.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: focused runtime/service tests, changed gate, docs formatting, diff whitespace, and Codex review.
- Edge cases checked: stale preserved runtime pins, explicit runtime override, wrapper-managed Bun path, node forced-reinstall pin preservation, doctor repair preservation, service audit explicit-pin suppression, and node host runtime path persistence.
- What you did **not** verify: live supervisor install/restart on launchd/systemd/schtasks from this branch.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: optional use of `--runtime-path`; existing installs keep current behavior unless reinstalled with the new flag or already carrying `OPENCLAW_DAEMON_RUNTIME_PATH`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: operator pins a runtime that is later deleted.
  - Mitigation: install validates the executable up front; future reinstall/doctor paths surface invalid preserved paths instead of silently switching runtimes.
- Risk: Bun remains incompatible with some channels.
  - Mitigation: Bun is still selected explicitly; Node remains the default/recommended runtime.
